blurb: Supports Albertans through employment supports, disability supports, prevention of elder abuse, community well-being, housing stability, health benefits, and financial assistance programs.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Albertans can meet their basic needs and live with financial stability
goals.description: Income support, health benefits, housing, and emergency assistance help Albertans maintain independence and security.

metrics.name: New affordable housing units + rent subsidies

supporting.name: Find out what support you're eligible for
supporting.desc: Get help navigating available programs and determining your eligibility for income support, disability benefits, health coverage, and other assistance
supporting.users: Albertans unsure which programs they qualify for

supporting.name: Get monthly income support for a severe disability (AISH)
supporting.desc: Monthly income and health benefits for persons with severe disabilities
supporting.users: Persons with severe disabilities limiting ability to earn

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act

supporting.name: Get income and employment support with a disability (ADAP)
supporting.desc: Monthly benefit up to $1,740 with $700/month employment income exemption — launching July 1, 2026
supporting.users: Persons with disabilities who can participate in employment

supporting.name: Get financial help to meet basic needs (Income Support)
supporting.desc: Financial assistance for individuals and families who cannot meet basic needs. Includes WorkFirst Alberta mandatory employment participation requirements for recipients expected to work.
supporting.users: Individuals/families who cannot meet basic needs

supporting.name: Get emergency financial assistance
supporting.desc: Crisis assistance for shelter, food, clothing, transportation
supporting.users: Albertans in crisis needing shelter, food, clothing

supporting.name: Get dental, optical, and prescription coverage
supporting.desc: Dental, optical, prescription, and emergency ambulance coverage for low-income adults
supporting.users: Low-income Albertans not on AISH or Income Support

supporting.name: Get health coverage for your children
supporting.desc: Health coverage for families with dependent children

supporting.name: Appeal a benefit decision
supporting.desc: Independent review of AISH, Income Support, or PDD benefit decisions

supporting.desc: Coverage for dental and eye care for seniors with low-to-moderate income

supporting.desc: Grants for home modifications to support aging in place

foundational_services.desc: Alberta Social Housing Corporation (ASHC) holds and manages 60,503+ social housing assets, distributing capital and operational funding to housing providers

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Albertans with disabilities and complex needs participate fully in community life
goals.description: Employment pathways, developmental disability supports, family services, and accessible transit enable participation and independence.

Invested in Career and Employment Information Services

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Alberta seniors and communities are safe, connected, and supported
goals.description: Community-based services, elder abuse prevention, FASD supports, and family programs strengthen resilience across the province.

metrics.name: Seniors' sense of belonging to community
Family and Community Support Services funding
Invested in elder abuse prevention initiatives

main_services.name: Get funding for community social programs

supporting.name: Apply for FCSS funding
supporting.desc: 80/20 funding partnership for preventive social programs run by municipalities

legislation.full_name: Family and Community Support Services Act

supporting.name: Apply for community capacity-building grants
supporting.desc: Grants to strengthen non-profit organizations

main_services.name: Age well and stay safe as a senior

supporting.name: Find healthy aging programs in your community
supporting.desc: Community-based supports for healthy aging and social engagement

supporting.name: Get connected to community health and wellness activities
supporting.desc: Integration of medical and community supports for wellbeing

supporting.name: Get non-medical home support
supporting.desc: Housekeeping, meal assistance, and daily living help

supporting.name: Book community transportation as a senior
supporting.desc: Assisted and volunteer transportation services

supporting.name: Report or get help with elder abuse
supporting.desc: Hotline and support services for seniors experiencing abuse

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Albertans have access to quality continuing care close to home
goals.description: Home care, supportive living, and long-term care are accessible, timely, and meet the needs of all Albertans, including Indigenous Peoples.

metrics.name: Clients moved to continuing care within 30 days
Home care hours provided to 140,000 clients
Increase direct care hours in care homes

supporting.name: Find continuing care options and compare homes
supporting.desc: Navigation portal to explore continuing care placements, costs, and vacancies
supporting.users: All Albertans seeking continuing care information

supporting.desc: Client-directed home care invoicing (2M+ hours, $67.2M)

main_services.name: Protect your rights and plan for the future

supporting.name: Make and register a personal directive
supporting.desc: Legal document naming someone to make decisions if you become incapacitated. Register it with the Personal Directives Registry for safekeeping.

supporting.desc: Court process to get decision-making authority for vulnerable adults
supporting.users: Families of adults who need decision-making support

supporting.desc: Medical evaluation of a person's decision-making ability

supporting.desc: Report concerns about abuse or mismanagement of affairs

supporting.desc: Guardianship and trusteeship services for vulnerable adults without private trustees

foundational_services.name: License and monitor continuing care homes
foundational_services.desc: Assisted Living Alberta licenses and monitors continuing care homes, developing and enforcing care standards

foundational_services.name: Provide data analytics and quality assurance
foundational_services.desc: Strategic Services and Digital Transformation unit provides data analytics, red tape reduction, quality assurance, and intergovernmental relations

cross_cutting: Nominate someone for a Minister's Seniors Service Award

mandate_legend.text: Continue implementation of housing strategy — remove barriers, incentivize construction, work with all levels of government
Work with Hospital & Surgical Health to transition non-acute patients to appropriate community or continuing care settings
Implement the Alberta Disability Assistance Program (ADAP) and create employment pathways for persons with disabilities
Expand and improve seniors lodges and continuing care facilities across the province
Launch new Assisted Living Alberta website to help Albertans navigate the continuing care system
Coordinate income-tested programs across government to streamline applications and reduce overlap
Work with Education to support students with complex needs transitioning to adult services (supporting)
Support health system restructuring through transition to Assisted Living Alberta (supporting)
Support improvements to emergency health services delivery (supporting)
Improve government procurement through 1GX implementation and category management (supporting)
Support delegation of bitumen royalty pipeline operations to appropriate entities (supporting)
